note: Claims carry no bare check date. A claim is promoted out of archive-derived only by naming a scope entry that a reviewer recorded against that paper in papers-en.json; everything else stays at the paper's baseline state however recently the file was touched.

contexts.label: Inherited GPX4 hypofunction and neurodegeneration
contexts.scale: patient, iPSC neuron, organoid and conditional mouse

contexts.label: Cancer cell state and therapy tolerance
contexts.scale: cultured carcinoma lines, xenograft and persister populations

contexts.label: Kidney ischaemia-reperfusion injury
contexts.scale: mouse in vivo and freshly isolated tubules

contexts.label: Organ preservation and transplantation
contexts.scale: porcine ex situ perfusion, declined human lungs and recipient plasma

contexts.label: Warfarin overdose and vitamin K reduction
Propagation of death through a contacting cell population

perturbations.label: FXT-001
perturbations.kind: membrane radical trap with iron binding

perturbations.label: Light-driven GPX4 degradation in a chosen cell

claims.claimScope: Membrane access, not catalytic rate, is the property lost in the R152H variant: the double-serine mutant keeps its fold and purified activity yet cannot rescue GPX4-null cells.

conditionVector.model: GPX4-null human cells, bicelles and phosphatidylserine giant vesicles
conditionVector.readout: membrane binding, fractionation, TIRF and survival

claims.confidenceBasis: Structural, biophysical and cellular rescue readouts agree, and a separation-of-function mutant dissociates catalysis from rescue.

claims.claimScope: Neuronal GPX4 loss or R152H hypofunction produces cortical and cerebellar degeneration in mice, with plasma neurofilament light as the biomarker that liproxstatin-1 lowers.

conditionVector.readout: serial MRI, Nissl, TUNEL, plasma neurofilament light

claims.confidenceBasis: Two neuron-specific genetic models agree, but the rescue is a short-term biomarker readout without recovery of volume, behaviour or survival.

claims.claimScope: Bicelle NMR and giant-vesicle binding are used to place the fin-loop at the membrane interface in a defined-lipid system.

conditionVector.readout: NMR chemical shift and vesicle binding

claims.confidenceBasis: Reconstituted lipid composition is simpler than a neuronal membrane, so binding is measured rather than residence time in situ.

claims.claimScope: Inducible neuron-restricted deletion is the causal control that connects the variant to degeneration in a mature brain.
claims.confidenceBasis: Conditional deletion is a strong genetic stress and is not equivalent to the hypomorphic patient allele.

claims.claimScope: Ferroptosis is unmasked by liproxstatin-1 withdrawal rather than by an added inducer, which is why the model reports a maintained dependence rather than an induced death.

conditionVector.model: patient iPSC neurons and forebrain organoids
conditionVector.readout: live-cell death imaging and organoid degeneration

claims.confidenceBasis: Early organoid culture depended on antioxidant support, so the baseline is a rescued state rather than an untreated one.

claims.claimScope: POR acts at the peroxidation step rather than at substrate supply: the global lipidome is essentially unchanged while four separate oxidation readouts fall together - BODIPY-C11 by flow cytometry, BODIPY-C11 by imaging, malondialdehyde, and species-resolved hydroperoxy- and hydroxy-PUFA-phosphatidylethanolamine.

conditionVector.readout: lipidomics, C11-BODIPY, malondialdehyde and targeted LC-MS

claims.confidenceBasis: Four independent oxidation readouts move together while the substrate pool does not. Cytosolic reactive oxygen species measured in parallel with H2-DCFDA were only modestly altered, and no direct electron acceptor is identified.

claims.claimScope: The data cannot separate a direct enzymatic role for POR from a global shift in cellular redox state; the double knockout only delays death rather than abolishing it.
claims.confidenceBasis: Two drug-processing explanations are excluded, but the positive mechanism is not resolved.

claims.claimScope: Targeted LC-MS of oxidised phosphatidylethanolamines is what places POR downstream of substrate supply.
claims.confidenceBasis: Failure to detect oxidised phosphatidylcholine is method- and model-limited, so absence is not evidence of absence.

claims.claimScope: Genome-wide CRISPR suppressor screening across two sensitisation states is the unbiased basis for naming POR at all.

conditionVector.perturbation: genome-wide knockout with ML210 and RSL3

claims.confidenceBasis: Calibrated against the known ACSL4 hit and reproduced across guides, clones and lineages.

claims.claimScope: One arm of the screen depends on exogenous fatty-acid loading, which is a sensitised state rather than a physiological lipid exposure.

conditionVector.perturbation: supplementation with six polyunsaturated fatty acids

claims.confidenceBasis: Oleic acid does not sensitise in the same way, which supports specificity but not physiological relevance.

claims.claimScope: Peroxisomal ether-lipid synthesis sets the polyunsaturated substrate pool that ferroptosis draws on; rescue converges on the sn-2 polyunsaturated chain rather than the ether bond.

conditionVector.perturbation: PEX, FAR1, AGPS or AGPAT3 loss with add-back

claims.confidenceBasis: Convergent hits across two screens with genetic add-back, though no oxidised ether-lipid species is shown to carry the lethal oxidation.

claims.claimScope: Xenografts that recur after GPX4 loss stay GPX4-null and instead lower ether lipids, AGPS and TMEM189, so resistance runs through lipid remodelling.

conditionVector.perturbation: combined GPX4 and ether-lipid pathway disruption
conditionVector.readout: tumour growth, GPX4 status and lipid content

claims.confidenceBasis: A heavily selected engineered system; it does not show that patient tumours adapt this way to clinical GPX4 inhibitors.

claims.claimScope: Lipidomic quantification of ether phospholipids is the measurement that distinguishes a substrate change from a defence change.
claims.confidenceBasis: Class-level abundance is measured; species-level oxidation in situ is not.

claims.claimScope: FSP1 suppresses ferroptosis in parallel with GPX4 by regenerating the radical-trapping form of coenzyme Q, and the effect requires myristoylation-dependent membrane localisation.

conditionVector.model: human cancer lines with GPX4 deleted or inhibited
conditionVector.perturbation: expression cloning, FSP1 loss and membrane-targeting mutants
conditionVector.readout: survival, lipid-radical probes and NAD(P)H consumption

claims.confidenceBasis: Cellular genetics and purified-protein liposome assays agree on the same requirement.

claims.claimScope: The defence operates without glutathione, which places it beside rather than downstream of the GPX4 axis in these cells.
claims.confidenceBasis: Parallelism is established in cultured cells under acute GPX4 loss, not across tissues.

claims.claimScope: Purified protein and liposome co-oxidation kinetics are what turn a genetic association into a chemical mechanism.
claims.confidenceBasis: A defined chemical system supports the proposed reaction independently of cellular context.

claims.claimScope: FSP1 reduces vitamin K to a hydroquinone that traps radicals in membranes, adding a second reducible substrate to the same enzyme.

conditionVector.model: cells with GPX4 deleted or inhibited and FENIX liposomes
conditionVector.perturbation: recombinant FSP1 with vitamin K forms, iFSP1, G2A mutant
conditionVector.readout: death, oxidised PE and liposome kinetics

claims.confidenceBasis: Chemistry, cellular genetics and a membrane-defective mutant agree.

claims.claimScope: Fsp1-null mice cannot be rescued from warfarin overdose by high-dose vitamin K, which identifies the warfarin-resistant reducing activity.

conditionVector.perturbation: warfarin overdose with supranutritional vitamin K

claims.confidenceBasis: Supranutritional dosing given before injury; this is pharmacology, not a dietary claim.

claims.claimScope: Vitamin K is supplied at nutritional excess, so protection is measured under supplementation rather than at endogenous concentrations.

conditionVector.model: hepatocyte-specific Gpx4 knockout mice and ischaemia-reperfusion

claims.confidenceBasis: The in vivo anti-ferroptotic arm was not tested on an Fsp1-null background.

claims.claimScope: The lethal oxidation signal narrows to four molecular species within a single phospholipid class: doubly and triply oxygenated diacyl phosphatidylethanolamines carrying arachidonoyl or adrenoyl chains at sn-2. Of 62 phosphatidylethanolamine species and 36 oxidisable ones, 2 were identified as precursors; of 57 oxygenated phosphatidylethanolamines, 4 were identified as signals.

conditionVector.model: cultured cells with RSL3 or Gpx4 loss and inducible Gpx4-knockout mouse kidney
conditionVector.perturbation: RSL3, genetic Gpx4 loss, arachidonic acid loading
conditionVector.readout: quantitative redox lipidomics of about 350 phospholipids

claims.confidenceBasis: Species-resolved lipidomics plus deuterated tracing and defined oxidised-lipid supplementation.

claims.claimScope: Entry of arachidonate and adrenate into phosphatidylethanolamine is ACSL4-dependent: supplementing arachidonic acid raises oxygenated esterified C20:4 and C22:4, and Acsl4-knockout cells accumulate less of both (72.2 +/- 27.0 and 28.2 +/- 8.0 against 199.3 +/- 26.2 and 137.8 +/- 77.7 pmol per micromole phospholipid, p < 0.01).

conditionVector.model: Pfa1 mouse embryonic fibroblasts, wild-type and Acsl4 knockout; MLE cells for the LPCAT3 arm
conditionVector.perturbation: arachidonic acid 2.5 micromolar for 16 h, then RSL3 100 nanomolar for 6 h; Acsl4 knockout; Lpcat3 knockdown
conditionVector.dose: RSL3 100 nanomolar (0.5-1.0 micromolar in MLE cells)
conditionVector.exposure: 6 h RSL3 after 16 h precursor loading
conditionVector.medium: DMEM with 10% fetal bovine serum; HITES medium with 10% serum for MLE cells
conditionVector.readout: oxygenated esterified C20:4 and C22:4 by LC-MS/MS after PAF-AH release, plus cell death
conditionVector.compartment: whole-cell phospholipid extract; no organelle resolution

claims.confidenceBasis: Species-resolved quantification of oxygenated esterified acyls in wild-type against Acsl4-knockout cells, in the same figure as the death readout.

correction.correctedBy: Claude Code round-4 primary-source pass (implementer, not an independent reviewer)
correction.why: Fig. 4 of this paper is titled "Oxygenated PE species are identified in ferroptotic GPX4 deficient cells and kidney of GPX4-deficient mice" and its five panels are all Gpx4-knockout cell or mouse-kidney measurements. It contains no ACSL4 or LPCAT3 arm. The claim's own condition vector already named ACSL4 and LPCAT3 loss, which is Fig. 2.
correction.alsoNarrowed: The previous wording said the lethal species "requires ACSL4 and LPCAT3 esterification". The LPCAT3 arm (Fig. 2e) is a death readout in Lpcat3-knockdown MLE cells; the paper reports no oxidised-PE measurement under Lpcat3 knockdown, so the lipidomic requirement is stated for ACSL4 only.

claims.claimScope: Quantitative redox lipidomics is the only readout in this set that names an individual oxidised species rather than reporting bulk oxidation.

conditionVector.readout: about 350 phospholipids and their oxidation products

claims.confidenceBasis: This measurement is what separates a named oxidised phospholipid from a probe signal.

claims.claimScope: RSL3-initiated oxidation is detected first in lysosomes, and liproxstatin-1 acts in part by inactivating lysosomal iron, which places an organelle at the start of the measured sequence.

conditionVector.model: cancer lines, primary sarcoma and PDAC cells, organoids and persisters
conditionVector.readout: clickable-probe localisation, iron probes, LiperFluo and C11-BODIPY

claims.confidenceBasis: A first-detected compartment in one inducer and probe set is not a universal organelle sequence for ferroptosis.

claims.claimScope: Activating lysosomal iron kills iron-rich CD44-high tumour cells including drug-tolerant persisters, with intranodal dosing in a metastasis model.
claims.confidenceBasis: Intranodal dosing is local delivery and does not establish a systemic treatment window.

claims.claimScope: Fentomycin-1 is a purpose-built activator of lysosomal iron, so the result is a designed chemical test of the proposed geography rather than an observation.
claims.confidenceBasis: A designed activator and a radical-trapping antioxidant give converging evidence for the same compartment.

claims.claimScope: Compartment-resolved iron probes are what turn a general iron dependence into a claim about where the iron is.
claims.confidenceBasis: Probe localisation reports where a signal is detected, not where the reaction is rate-limiting.

claims.claimScope: Lipid peroxidation and death spread to immediate neighbours in a distance-dependent way that requires alpha-catenin-dependent contacts and extracellular iron.

conditionVector.perturbation: patterned blue light degrading GPX4 in single cells
conditionVector.readout: C11-BODIPY oxidation and death timing versus distance

claims.confidenceBasis: Single-cell initiation plus reconstitution between pure contacting bilayers isolates the transfer from bulk medium effects.

claims.claimScope: Propagation is measured in a cultured monolayer and in reconstituted bilayers, not in tissue.
claims.confidenceBasis: A monolayer is a controlled geometry, not a tissue with stroma, flow or immune cells.

claims.claimScope: Death timing against distance from the initiating cell is the measurement that makes propagation a claim rather than an impression.

conditionVector.perturbation: patterned light in single cells or small groups

claims.confidenceBasis: Spatially controlled initiation is what separates propagation from synchronous bulk induction.

claims.claimScope: Optogenetic GPX4 degradation replaces a dish-wide inducer, which is the design change that makes a propagation question answerable.
claims.confidenceBasis: The perturbation is spatially defined, so neighbouring-cell death cannot be explained by direct induction.

claims.claimScope: An early, transient window of lipid oxidation is identified in human liver transplant recipients, which is when an intervention would have to act.

conditionVector.model: plasma from 116 human liver transplant recipients

claims.confidenceBasis: Circulating oxidation markers in patients are an association; they do not measure ferroptosis in graft tissue.

claims.claimScope: FXT-001 added to the preservation or perfusion circuit improves ex situ function of porcine liver and lung grafts and of declined human lungs.

conditionVector.model: porcine liver reperfused ex situ for six hours, porcine lung for eight hours, five declined human lungs as split pairs
conditionVector.readout: transaminases, bile production, wet-to-dry ratio, compliance and CT density

claims.confidenceBasis: Ex situ perfusion of a declined human organ is not a clinical trial and no recipient outcome is measured.

claims.claimScope: Split left-and-right perfusion of the same declined human lung is the design that gives an internal control at organ scale.

conditionVector.perturbation: FXT-001 in one side of a split pair

claims.confidenceBasis: A paired design controls for donor variability but the sample is five organs and the endpoint is ex situ function.

claims.claimScope: FXT-001 both traps radicals in membranes and binds iron, so protection cannot be attributed to a single chemical activity.
claims.confidenceBasis: A dual-activity compound cannot separate radical trapping from iron binding without a separation-of-function analogue.

claims.claimScope: Hydroxyoestradiol derivatives directly trap lipid radicals and can be regenerated by FSP1, which makes an endogenous steroid part of the membrane antioxidant network.

conditionVector.perturbation: oestradiol derivatives at about ten micromolar

claims.confidenceBasis: Defined chemical kinetics plus cellular genetics, though the cellular concentration used is far above physiological oestradiol.

claims.claimScope: Tubule-specific Gpx4, Fsp1 and Esr1 deletion together with ovariectomy connect the sex difference in kidney injury to two separable oestradiol routes.

conditionVector.model: male and female mouse kidney ischaemia-reperfusion and isolated tubules
conditionVector.perturbation: ovariectomy, tubule-specific deletions, oestradiol before injury
conditionVector.readout: creatinine, urea and synchronised tubular death imaging

claims.confidenceBasis: Strong mouse genetics with only small human sample sets; the human arm does not measure ferroptosis in patients.

claims.claimScope: ESR1 suppresses pro-ferroptotic ether-lipid enzymes in female tubules, which reproduces in a physiological setting the substrate-pool control first shown in cancer lines.
claims.confidenceBasis: Expression-level replication in a different tissue; the oxidised species themselves are not re-measured here.

claims.claimScope: Derivatives are applied at about ten micromolar in cells and ten milligrams per kilogram before injury, so the result is pharmacology on top of an endogenous mechanism.
claims.confidenceBasis: Pre-injury dosing at supraphysiological concentration does not establish that endogenous oestradiol acts at the same site.

claims.claimScope: The work proposes that dihydroorotate dehydrogenase reduces coenzyme Q in the inner mitochondrial membrane in parallel with mitochondrial GPX4.

conditionVector.model: cancer lines stratified by GPX4 expression
conditionVector.perturbation: brequinar often at 500 micromolar with uridine supplied
conditionVector.readout: mitochondrial C11-BODIPY, coenzyme Q redox ratio and 4-hydroxynonenal

claims.confidenceBasis: A 2023 Matters Arising argues the brequinar concentrations also inhibit FSP1, so the attribution to DHODH is contested and the authors' Reply disagrees.

claims.claimScope: Brequinar and sulfasalazine dosing in xenografts and patient-derived xenografts is presented as a GPX4-low therapeutic window.

conditionVector.model: xenografts and patient-derived xenografts in immunodeficient mice
conditionVector.perturbation: brequinar 30 mg/kg every three days, sulfasalazine 100 mg/kg daily

claims.confidenceBasis: Dosing is in immunodeficient hosts and, given the 2023 dispute, the proposed drug selectivity and GPX4 stratification are not established as translatable.

claims.claimScope: Brequinar is used at concentrations that the later Matters Arising identifies as also inhibiting FSP1, which is the centre of the dispute.

conditionVector.perturbation: brequinar often at 500 micromolar with uridine rescue

claims.confidenceBasis: Uridine supplementation removes the pyrimidine deficit but does not address off-target FSP1 inhibition.

claims.claimScope: At the concentrations used, a DHODH-dependent and an FSP1-dependent explanation of the same protection cannot be separated by these experiments.

conditionVector.readout: mitochondrial lipid oxidation and coenzyme Q redox ratio

claims.confidenceBasis: This is the explicit content of the 2023 Matters Arising; the record shows both positions rather than adjudicating between them.
